methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $57M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Zama is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Rand Hindi and Pascal Paillier that has raised $57M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies—including Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—alongside one public comparable (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $57M invested base, implying a $108M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a $5.0B median valuation, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous capital intensity and market positioning within early-stage AI.

The two methods produce materially divergent signals. Triangulation across both approaches supports a range of $92M (floor) to $5.8B (ceiling), with a median of $2.6B. This wide band reflects genuine uncertainty: stage-progression anchors to near-term financing patterns, while peer-median is heavily influenced by the composition and valuation disparity of the comparable set, particularly the outsized positions of Safe Superintelligence and the gap to lower-valued peers. Resolution requires clarity on Zama's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and differentiation within its specific AI subsector.
